title: schizoglossum bidens subsp. hirtum
intro: Schizoglossum bidens subsp. hirtum is a species of flowering plant in the family Asclepiadaceae, commonly known as the "hairy-leaved Schizoglossum." This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The roots and leaves of Schizoglossum bidens subsp. hirtum are used in herbal preparations, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, providing health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as aiding in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can be used externally as a poultice or ointment.
